THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 2.5 MILES LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE.

*** 1 FATAL *** ENTIRE NORTHERN HALF OF THE COUNTY...NORTH OF HEMPHILL IS SHUT DOWN. FLOODING IS WIDESPREAD AND ROADS ARE CLOSED. NUMEROUS HOMES AND BUSINESSES FLOODED THROUGHOUT THE NORTHERN HALF OF THE COUNTY. 1 CONFIRMED FATALITY FROM FLOODING WHICH WAS VEHICLE RELATED.

EXTENSIVE FLOODING CONTINUES ACROSS MUCH OF THE PARISH. MANY ROADS REMAIN FLOODED AND CLOSED DUE TO HIGH/RISING WATER. SEVERAL ROADS AND BRIDGES ARE WASHED OUT THROUGHOUT THE PARISH.

AN EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 100 TO 110 MPH SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES NEAR HWY 3. 2 HOUSES ALONG HWY 3 SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE. THE TORNADO TRACKED MAINLY THROUGH WOODED AREAS SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES. THE PATH WAS 450 YARDS WIDE AND 5 MILES LONG AND WAS ON THE GROUND FROM 731 PM TO 741 PM.

AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILES WEST OF ATLANTA ALONG GUM SPRINGS ROAD. THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 400 YARDS WIDED AT ITS WIDEST POINT WHICH OCCURRED ALONG GUM SPRINGS ROAD WHERE A BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED WITH METAL STREWN INTO TREES DUE EAST OF THE BARN. ONE HOUSE SUSTAINED SHINGLE DAMAGE REQUIRING TARPS ON THE ROOF. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 6.5 MILE 4 MINUTE PATH WHICH ENDED ALONG STATE ROUTE 1228. TREES ALSO FELL IN ATLANTA AROUND A SCHOOL.

ONE HOUSE SUSTAINED SHINGLE DAMAGE REQUIRING TARPS ON THE ROOF. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 6.5 MILE 4 MINUTE PATH WHICH ENDED ALONG STATE ROUTE 1228. TREES ALSO FELL IN ATLANTA AROUND A SCHOOL.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED ALONG STATE ROUTE 124 WITH A WIDTH OF 200 YARDS AND A 7 MILE LONG TRACK. MANY LARGE TREES WERE DOWNED ONTO HWY 124 INCLUDING 1 ON A CAR INJURING THE DRIVER. THE PATH CROSSED T WELLS ROAD SOUTH OF HWY 124 AND ENDED 2 MILES SOUTH OF HWY 124 ALONG ALBRITTON RD SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES ALONG ITS PATH. NO HOUSES WERE DAMAGED FROM THIS TORNADO...HOWEVER...SEVERAL OF THE LARGE TREES SURVEYED FELL NEXT TO HOUSES. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 5 MINUTES.

NWS SURVEY AND EYEWITNESS REPORTS CONFIRMED THAT AN EF0 ROPE TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF PICKENS ON THE EVENING OF APRIL 9 2008. THE TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM THE TIME A CALL WAS MADE FROM THE RINGGOLD FIRE DEPARTMENT TO THE PICKENS FIRE DEPARTMENT NOTIFYING THEM OF THE SIGHTING AND FROM RADAR. ONLY A FEW TREES WERE DAMAGED IN AN UNINHABITED AREA SOUTH OF PICKENS. THE PATH IS ESTIMATED AT 1 MILE LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE. EYEWITNESS REPORTS ESTIMATE THE TORNADO ON THE GROUND FOR 3 MINUTES.

NWS SURVEY AND EYEWITNESS ACCOUNTS CONFIRMED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN THE EVENING OF APRIL 9 2008 WEST OF HOCHATOWN IN AN UNIHABITED AREA ALONG CEDAR CREEK TRAIL. A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF AND UPROOTED IN A 150 YARD WIDE PATH THAT IS ESTIMATED TO BE 1 MILE LONG. THE TORNADO IS ESTIMATED TO HAVE BEEN ON THE GROUND FOR 2 MINUTES.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES FROM 812 AM TO 814 AM. APPROXIMATELY 20 STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED ALONG THE TRACK INCLUDING 2 BRICK BUILDINGS AND SEVERAL BUSINESSES AND HOMES. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE TRACK. 3 TRACTOR TRAILERS WERE OVERTURNED ON I-30 SHUTTING DOWN PART OF THE INTERSTATE AND INJURING 1 DRIVER. DAMAGE WAS ALSO REPORTED ON THE LONESTAR ARMY AMMUNITION DEPOT. THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 200 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT

AN EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 95 TO 105 MPH OCCURRED NEAR UMPIRE ARKANSAS BEGINNING NEAR THE DAVIS CEMETARY ALONG PINKERTON ROAD. THE TORNADO DESTROYED 2 BARNS AND DAMAGED A MOBILE HOME FROM A FALLING TREE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 4 MILE TRACK ENDING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PARSONS ROAD AND HILL LANE. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FROM 903 AM UNTIL 908 AM AND WAS 50 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT.
EAST OF UMPIRE...TWO BARNS DESTROYED...DAMAGE TO TWO HOUSES AND NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN.
NWS STORM SURVEY ON MAY 3 2008 AND EYEWITNESSES CONFIRM 1 INCH HAIL OCCURRED AT THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 69 AND I-20 NORTH OF TYLER. CARS PULLED UNDER THE INTERCHANGE TO AVOID THE HAIL.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS TORNADO ON THE GROUND NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 43 AND FM 1251. REPORTED THAT THE TORNADO HIT HIS HOUSE.

AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED NORTHEAST OF HENDERSON TEXAS ALONG HWY 43 FROM 1121 AM TO 1126 AM. DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A METAL BUILDING AND HOME AT A CONSTRUCTION COMPANY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH WHICH ENDED 5.5 MILES EAST ALONG FM 1251. A HAY BARN WAS PARTIALLY DESTROYED AND SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED AROUND A HOME ALONG FM 1251. THE PATH WAS 100 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT.

AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 241 AND MOVED SOUTHEASTWARD ALONG COUNTY ROAD 224 BEFORE LIFTING. NUMEROUS HOMES WERE DAMAGED MAINLY FROM FALLING TREES. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH WHICH ENDED 5 MILES SOUTHEAST ALONG CR 224. SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ONTO HOMES IN ALONG CR 241 AND CR 248. THE TORNADO WAS 200 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT AND ON THE GROUND UNTIL 1203 PM.

STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 90 TO 100 MPH THAT OCCURREDFROM APPROXIMATELY 1250 AM. NUMEROUS HOUSES WERE DAMAGED THROUGHOUT WINNSBORO AND SURROUNDING AREAS FROM FALLING TREES. SOME OF THEM SEVERELY DAMAGED. AND AS A RESULT OF THE HUNDREDS OF TREES DOWNED...WATER LINES INTO THE AREA WERE BROKEN AND POWER OUTAGES WERE WIDESPREAD. NUMEROUS CARS WERE ALSO SEVERELY DAMAGED IN WINNSBORO DUE TO FALLING TREES. SEVERAL CATTLE WERE KILLED WEST OF WINNSBORO NR THE HOPKINS AND WOOD COUNTY LINE FROM FALLING TREES.

AN EF1 TORNADO TRACK OCCURRED ON THE NORTHWEST SIDE OF WINNSBORO ALONG HWY 11 AND TRACKED EASTWARD CROSSING GREENWOOD STREET WHERE A MOBILE HOME SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EASTWARD CROSSING HWY 37 WHERE SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. THE TORNADO HEADED EASTWARD CROSSING FM 1448 SNAPPING AND UPROOTED A FEW TREES BEFORE LIFTING. THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT.

RESIDUAL FLOODING FROM EARLIER RAIN CONTINUING IN MONROE AND WEST MONROE. SEVERAL ROADS STILL FLOODED IN THE MONROE AND WEST MONROE AREA. SOME EVACUATIONS BEING CONSIDERED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT AND EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.

EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS NATIONAL GUARD CALLED INTO EVACUATE PEOPLE FROM THEIR HOMES. MOST OCCURED IN THE TANGLEWOOD AREA. APPROXIMATELY 64 PEOPLE PUT SHELTERS DUE TO FLOODING. NUMEROUS HOUSES AND CARS FLOODED.

S/O REPORTS NUMEROUS ROADS FLOODED THROUGHOUT THE NORTHERN AND SOUTHERN SECTIONS OF THE COUNTY.

A ROOF WAS RIPPED OFF OF A METAL OUTBUILDING AND INSULATION WAS STREWN INTO TREES AND ON THE GROUND FOR 0.25 MILES. MINOR DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A TRAILER 0.12 MILE SOUTHEAST NEAR WHERE THE EF0 TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN. SEVERAL TREES WERE DAMAGED ABOUT 0.12 MILES NORTHWEST. THE TOTAL PATH OF THIS TORNADO WAS 0.25 MILES LONG AND APPROXIMATELY 75 YARDS WIDE.

SEVERAL TREES WERE KNOCKED OVER AND A POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED JUST EAST OF GOODWILL RD ALONG ELMO BURTON LOOP. NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES WERE ALSO THROWN IN VARIOUS DIRECTIONS. THE EF0 TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 75 YARDS WIDE AND .50 MILES LONG.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN RURAL JACKSON PARISH NORTHEAST OF QUITMAN NEAR JOINER ROAD. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY .75 MILES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR MT. ZION ROAD. A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 40 YARD WIDE PATH.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN RURAL JACKSON PARISH SOUTHEAST OF RUSTON ALONG RISER ROAD. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 3.1 MILES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE LINCOLN COUNTY LINE ALONG RISER ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 50 YARD WIDE PATH. LARGE BRANCHES WERE ALSO BROKEN ALONG THE TRACK.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN LINCOLN PARISH JUST SOUTH OF THE CITY LIMITS OF RUSTON. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 0.25 MILES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE RUSTON CITY LIMIT ALONG RISER ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 50 YARD WIDE PATH INCLUDING SOME LARGE TREES.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN NEAR HWY 33. POSSIBLE TORNADO

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN LINCOLN PARISH NORTH OF DUBACH NEAR HAYHOUSE RD. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 0.25 MILES. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED IN A THICK FORESTED AREA ALONG HAYHOUSE ROAD ALONG A 40 YARD WIDE PATH.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN UNION PARISH LA WEST OF BERNICE NEAR BUSBY ROAD. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 0.25 MILES. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN A ALONG A 40 YARD WIDE PATH.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN LA SALLE PARISH NORTHWEST OF JENA NEAR HWY 84. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED INCLUDING ONE ONTO A MOBILE HOME. THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 1.25 MILES LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN CALDWELL PARISH NORTHEAST OF OLLA ALONG WHITE ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND SEVERAL POWER LINES WERE DOWNED ALONG WHITE ROAD. THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 0.25 MILES LONG AND 30 YARDS WIDE.
SEVERAL TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS THE CITY. SOME HOUSES WITH ROOF DAMAGE. POWER IS OUT TO MUCH OF THE CITY.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHERN CALDWELL PARISH NEAR THE OUACHITA RIVER ALONG LENARD ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED IN A FIELD ALONG LENARD ROAD AND IN A FORESTED AREA NEAR THE END OF THE TRACK. THE PATH WAS APPROXIMATELY 0.25 MILES LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN UNION PARISH LA NORTHWEST OF STERLINGTON NEAR SPENCER. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 3.50 MILES. SEVERAL TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED. ONE LARGE TREE WAS KNOCKED OVER ON AN OUTBUILDING ALONG ARKANSAS ROAD.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN UNION COUNTY AR IN EAST OF STRONG ALONG NORTH ROAD. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN THE SWAMP ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE ROAD ALONG A PATH OF APPROXIMATELY 0.25 MILES.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN UNION COUNTY AR IN THE TOWN OF HUTTIG. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 0.50 MILES. SEVERAL TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED AND A HOUSE IN HUTTIG AR SUSTAINED SOME ROOF DAMAGE.
SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND KNOCKED OVER ALONG A STRETCH ALONG STATE ROUTE 783 BETWEEN EDGEFIELD AND HALL SUMMIT. THE PATH WAS EF0 DAMAGE ABOUT 40 YARDS WIDE AND .25 MILES LONG.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1.5 MILES NORTHWEST OF MINDEN LA JUST WEST OF THE MINDEN AIRPORT ALONG METHODIST CAMP ROAD. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES BEFORE DISAPATING NEAR THE KISATCHIE NATIONAL FOREST. A FEW TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED AND DAMAGE OCCURRED TO SOME POWER LINES ALONG THE 40 YARD WIDE PATH.
EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED FROM 13 MI SW OF NATCHEZ TO 4 MI SW OF NATCHEZ. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF SEVERAL TREES DOWN AND SNAPPED ALONG A 9 MILE PATH. PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED FROM 11 MI NNW OF WINNFIELD TO 16 MI N OF WINNFIELD. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF SEVERAL TREES DOWN. PATH LENGTH WAS 6.5 MI AND PATH WIDTH WAS 30 YARDS. TORNADO CONTINUED INTO JACKSON PARISH.
EF0 TORNADO CONTINUED INTO JACKSON PARISH FROM WINN PARISH. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF A FEW TREES UPROOTED. PATH LENGTH IN JACKSON PARISH WAS ONE HALF MILE AND PATH WIDTH WAS 30 YARDS.
EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED FROM 1 MI SE OF MONTGOMERY TO 4 MI ENE OF MONTGOMERY. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. PATH LENGTH WAS 4 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.

AN EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED NEAR HWY 77 AND MOVED NORTH APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES. A STORAGE BUILDING WAS DESTROYED AND A CARPORT WAS LIFTED FROM A HOUSE AND DROPPED IN THE YARD ALONG COUNTY ROAD 4798. MULITPLE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR DOWNED ALONG THE 100 YARD WIDE PATH.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED IN THE HUFFINES AND SMYRNA AREAS ALONG FM 1841.
POWER LINES DOWNED SOUTH BOUND FROM SMITH COUNTY ON FM 346.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 69 AND FM 344 JUST EAST OF BULLARD WHERE IT SNAPPED TREES IN A WOODED AREA. THE STORM THEN MOVED ALONG FM 344 WHERE IT DOWNED ANOTHER TREE BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF FM 344 AND INWOOD DR. THE STORM TRACK WAS ABOUT 0.5 MILES LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE.
SEVERAL PECAN TREES SNAPPED OFF ON WEMPLE RD. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE RED RIVER WEST OF US HWY 71 IN A FIELD. A SMALL TIN OUTBUILDING AND SOME TREES ALONG THE LEVEE WERE DAMAGED. THE PATH AS 0.33 MILES LONG AND APPROXIMATELY 75 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 1 MINUTE. START 32.2429, -93.4572

AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE RED RIVER WEST OF US HWY 71 IN A FIELD. A SMALL TIN OUTBUILDING AND SOME TREES ALONG THE LEVEE WERE DAMAGED. THE PATH AS 0.33 MILES LONG AND APPROXIMATELY 75 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 1 MINUTE. START 32.2429, -93.4572

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN A QUARTER MILE SOUTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF GOODWILL RD AND PARISH RD 117. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED AND HOMES DAMAGED. NUMEROUS LARGE OAK TREES WERE SNAPPED AND TWISTED. PATH LENGTH--0.2 MILE. PATH WIDTH--50 YARDS. RATING--EF1.
DAMAGE REPORTED ON GOODWILLE RD, SAVANNA LN AND FULLER ROAD. AROUND 30 TREES DOWN AND SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED.SOME HOMES HAVE ROOF DAMAGE.
DAMAGE REPORTED ON GOODWILLE RD, SAVANNA LN AND FULLER ROAD. AROUND 30 TREES DOWN AND SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED.SOME HOMES HAVE ROOF DAMAGE.
SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED.

*** 1 INJ *** SEVERAL MODULAR HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. ONE MAN RECEIVED MINOR INJURIES AS HE WAS TEMPORARILY TRAPPED IN HIS HOME WHEN THE STORM STRUCK. 2 BRICK HOMES IN THE IMMEDIATE AREA SUFFERED SEVERE ROOF DAMAGE. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE PATH OF THE STORM AT THIS LOCATION WAS 300 YARDS WIDE.
A TORNADO CAUSED EF1 DAMAGE ON WILBURN RD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE PATH OF THE STORM WAS 100 YARDS WIDE.
ALONG HWY 127...TRAILERS DAMAGED IN SUMMERFIELD.
A TORNADO CAUSED EF0 DAMAGE ON BONEYARD RD. A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED. THE PATH WAS 50 YARDS WIDE.

AN EF0 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF APPROXIMATELY 65 MPH OCCURRED ON THE EAST SIDE OF CR 4315 NEAR A CEMETARY. SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND BRANCHES WERE DOWNED...ONE ON A HOUSE. A LARGE METAL ROOF WAS PEELED FROM A BARN AND INSULATION WAS THROWN INTO NEARBY TREES. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.4 MILES OR ABOUT 2 MINUTES. THE PATH WAS APPROX 50 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO LIFTED ALONG CR 4350.
